Nvidia Q3 earnings, Fed commentary: What to Watch Asking for a Trend anchor Josh Lipton details the key events investors will be watching tomorrow, Wednesday, November 20. Nvidia (NVDA) takes the spotlight as it prepares to release its third quarter earnings results, with market participants eager to see if the company can maintain its growth trajectory. Other notable companies reporting include TJX Companies (TJX), Palo Alto Networks (PANW), Target (TGT), Snowflake (SNOW), Williams-Sonoma (WSM), and NIO (NIO). Meanwhile, the financial world will hear commentary from Federal Reserve governors Lisa Cook, Michelle Bowman, and Boston Fed President Susan Collins. Their speeches will provide insights into the central banks monetary policy and economic outlooks. Snowflake shares surge on rosy forecast, AI deal with Anthropic The company, whose shares have fallen 35% this year, is on track for its best day since going public in 2020.  The Bozeman, Montana-based companys results come months after Sridhar Ramaswamy took over as CEO, having previously spearheaded Snowflake’s AI strategy as SVP of AI, and analysts have been since watching how the firms AI efforts are progressing under the new leadership.  Snowflake announced a partnership with Anthropic on Wednesday, which will allow its customers to enhance their AI applications using Anthropics large language models on Snowflakes cloud-based data platforms.
